headline: Retrieval infrastructure and security techniques broke out together this month while agent deployment moved into production.
body: A cluster of retrieval and security techniques broke out simultaneously: vector search, semantic chunking, and HNSW led on indexing, while vulnerability detection, tenant isolation, guardrails, and prompt-injection defense rose on the security side. Generative AI improved across the board alongside workflow automation. On the agent front, Spotify reported running Claude agents across more than 20 million lines of code to generate prototypes in hours [7], Cursor shipped an iOS app driven by always-on cloud agents [5], and Google Cloud demonstrated a production multi-agent system built on ADK, MCP, and Gemini 3 Flash [9]. Frontier model news included OpenAI's GPT-5.6 release, Anthropic's Mythos, and Apple price increases, with cost and billing pressures in focus [6].

cited_items.title: Mythos is Back, OpenAI Releases GPT 5.6, Apple’s Price Increases
How Spotify runs agents across 20M+ lines of code, with Niklas Gustavsson
Build a multi-agent system using ADK & MCP
